headline.purpose: Dialog titles and major section headers.

title.purpose: Toolbar title, compact section labels, and panel headings.

body.purpose: Default UI text, menus, controls, and dialog copy.

mono.purpose: DOT code, code-adjacent values, and technical text.

label.purpose: Form labels, compact metadata, and scan-friendly control labels.

shadows.name: hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Interactive lift for hoverable or raised controls, menus, snackbars, and cards.

motion.name: state-linear
motion.purpose: Immediate state transitions for primitive controls.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Square, dense command button for toolbar and dialog actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 4px; padding: 4px 16px; border: 2px solid #000; border-radius: 0; background: #fff; color: #000; font-family: "Space Grotesk", system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.2; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; cursor: pointer; transition: background 80ms linear, color 80ms linear; } .ds-btn-primary:hover, .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { background: #000; color: #fff; outline: 2px solid #000; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Icon Button
components.description: Fixed square toolbar control for familiar graph and editor tools.
components.html: <button class="ds-icon-button" aria-label="Zoom in"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M11 4h2v7h7v2h-7v7h-2v-7H4v-2h7V4z" fill="currentColor"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-icon-button { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 40px; height: 40px; padding: 0; border: 2px solid #000; border-radius: 0; background: #fff; color: #000; cursor: pointer; transition: background 80ms linear, color 80ms linear; } .ds-icon-button svg { width: 24px; height: 24px; } .ds-icon-button:hover, .ds-icon-button:focus-visible { background: #000; color: #fff; outline: 2px solid #000; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Sharp, plain text field for settings and code-adjacent values.
components.css: .ds-field { display: inline-grid; gap: 4px; font-family: "Space Grotesk", system-ui, sans-serif; color: #000; } .ds-label { font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.4; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; } .ds-input { width: 160px; padding: 4px 8px; border: 1px solid #000; border-radius: 0; background: #fff; color: #000; font-family: "Space Grotesk", system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 400; outline: none; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #000; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Switch
components.description: Compact binary control for editor settings and panel toggles.
components.css: .ds-switch { position: relative; width: 32px; height: 16px; border: 1px solid #000; border-radius: 0; background: #4ed1f8; padding: 0; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 80ms linear; } .ds-switch-thumb { position: absolute; top: 1px; left: 1px; width: 12px; height: 12px; background: #000; transform: translateX(16px); transition: transform 80ms linear; } .ds-switch:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #000; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: App Bar
components.description: Single-row command surface that keeps the editor controls visible without competing with the graph.
components.html: Graphviz Visual Editor
components.css: .ds-appbar { width: 100%; background: #fff; color: #000; border-bottom: 2px solid #000; } .ds-toolbar { display: flex; align-items: center; height: 56px; gap: 16px; padding: 0 24px; } .ds-title { flex: 1; font-family: "Space Grotesk", system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.3; } .ds-icon-button { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 40px; height: 40px; padding: 0; border: 2px solid #000; border-radius: 0; background: #fff; color: #000; } .ds-icon-button svg { width: 24px; height: 24px; } .ds-btn-primary { padding: 4px 16px; border: 2px solid #000; border-radius: 0; background: #fff; color: #000; font-family: "Space Grotesk", system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; }

components.name: Dialog Surface
components.description: Focused editing or confirmation layer with structural depth.
components.html:
Export as SVG

Save the current graph as a standalone SVG file.
components.css: .ds-dialog { width: 360px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 16px; padding: 24px; border: 2px solid #000; border-radius: 0; background: #fff; color: #000; box-shadow: 8px 8px 0 #000; font-family: "Space Grotesk", system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-dialog h2 { margin: 0; font-size: 24px; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.15; } .ds-dialog p { margin: 0; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.4; } .ds-btn-primary { align-self: flex-start; padding: 4px 16px; border: 2px solid #000; border-radius: 0; background: #fff; color: #000; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; }

narrative.overview:
graphviz-visual-editor is a fast, sleek, chic modeling workspace for humans supervising agent-generated Graphviz output. The user is watching diagrams arrive through MCP, checking the DOT source, and stepping in when a model needs correction. The interface must make that supervision feel immediate: graph, code, sync state, and errors are always close to the user's eye and hand.

The visual system keeps the existing brutalist discipline but aims for the cooler, sharper version of a modern Mermaid-style diagram editor. The canvas can be spacious and quiet; the chrome stays exacting, monochrome, square, and dense. Polish comes from alignment, typography, rhythm, and component consistency, not decoration.

The system explicitly rejects MUI / Material Design softness, Tailwind UI SaaS polish, rounded corners, soft gradients, over-animated dashboards, and anything that makes the editor feel like a marketing page instead of a working modeling surface.
keyCharacteristics: Monochrome product chrome with one cyan state accent.
Large graph canvas paired with immediate DOT access.
Dense, predictable controls for long modeling sessions.
Square geometry, strong borders, and hard offset depth.
Light mode as the current baseline, with dark mode expected later.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Cyan is the single product accent and should occupy less than 10% of any screen.

rules.name: The Canvas Exception Rule
rules.body: Graph content and graph selections may use their own colors; editor chrome may not borrow those colors for decoration.

rules.name: The Code-Is-Live Rule
rules.body: DOT source is not documentation; it is the editable model. Treat code surfaces as primary workspace, not secondary support.

rules.name: The Flat-Until-Activated Rule
rules.body: A surface is flat at rest unless it is a popup or dialog. Elevation must indicate state, stack order, or temporary focus.

dos: Do make agent output inspectable through visible graph state, DOT source, sync state, and parse errors.
Do preserve both direct manipulation and direct DOT editing.
Do use Electric Draft Cyan only for active, selected, focused, or checked state.
Do keep controls dense, aligned, and predictable for long modeling sessions.
Do use Space Grotesk for UI and JetBrains Mono for code or code-adjacent metadata.
Do keep every corner sharp at 0 radius.
Do keep motion immediate and purposeful: 80ms linear for existing primitive states.
donts: Don't use MUI / Material Design patterns, including soft corners, floating paper stacks, and generic material spacing.
Don't use Tailwind UI SaaS patterns, marketing-page composition, hero metrics, or repetitive card grids.
Don't use rounded corners, soft gradients, gradient text, blurred shadows, or decorative glassmorphism.
Don't add over-animated dashboards with bouncing curves or choreographed page-load sequences.
Don't introduce extra accent colors into editor chrome. Graph content is the exception, not the rule.
Don't hide code access, errors, or edit affordances behind modal-first flows.
